findings.summary: OSS default deployment applies no API authentication: get_dependencies() returns empty unless SESSION_API_KEY is set and DefaultUserAuth.get_user_id() always returns None, so any client reaching the port can provision code-executing sandboxes and read stored git tokens.

Approval gates are disabled by default: _select_confirmation_policy returns NeverConfirm() whenever confirmation_mode is off (the headless/CLI default) and security_analyzer defaults to None, so agent shell exec, git force-push, PR merge, and cross-repo writes run with no human-in-the-loop.

FileSecretsStore (the OSS default) persists provider PATs and custom secrets to secrets.json in plaintext via model_dump_json(expose_secrets=True) with no encryption at rest, contradicting the remit's encrypt-at-rest requirement.

Setting RUNTIME=local/process selects ProcessSandboxService, which spawns the agent server as a host subprocess with no container isolation, giving agent-generated code full host filesystem/network access in violation of the sandbox boundary the remit mandates.

LocalhostCORSMiddleware.is_allowed_origin() returns True for any origin when permitted_cors_origins is unset (the default), while allow_credentials=True, allowing a malicious site to make credentialed cross-origin requests to the API with only a logged warning.

Unbounded-consumption controls are weak by default: max_budget_per_task defaults to None (no cost cap) and the only HTTP throttle is an in-memory, per-process, IP-keyed InMemoryRateLimiter that is ineffective across workers and resets on restart.

Live secrets are embedded in URLs: the sandbox session API key is placed in the VSCode URL query string (?tkn=...) and the Tavily API key is placed in the MCP proxy URL, exposing them to logs, referrers, and history.

The app server records events durably but implements no runtime detection, alerting, or halt for the remit's escalation conditions (secret detected in logs/trajectory, sandbox-escape attempt), leaving oversight log-only over consequential capabilities.

get_default_encryption_keys writes a generated master key to workspace_dir/.keys (expose_secrets=True) and reads .jwt_secret in plaintext, storing the key material beside the data it protects with no external key management.
